forecast-period.text: A weak ridge of high pressure begins to move across SA today ahead of a cold front moving across the south on Friday. Another weak high pressure system moves across from the west over the weekend.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural areas and western districts. Isolated light showers about southern coasts and ranges during the morning. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Variable winds

Morning fog patches about the agricultural areas and southern pastoral districts. Isolated showers about western coasts and the southern agricultural area, becoming more frequent about the southeast during the day. Maximum temperatures near average. Variable winds becoming northwesterly ahead of a westerly change moving across western and central districts.

Showers about the agricultural areas, western coasts and south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures near average. West to southwesterly winds extending throughout, fresh at times about the coasts.

text.p: Isolated showers about the southern agricultural area, with showers and possible thunderstorms developing about the west during the day. Maximum temperatures near to below average, tending slightly above average in he northwest. West to southwesterly winds, tending north to northeasterly across western districts.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Saturday: Light falls across the southern pastoral districts. Widespread 1 to 5 mm elsewhere, increasing to 5 to 15 mm across western coasts and parts of the Flinders and Mount Lofty Ranges.`
